title: Compound index audit treats independent field usages as proof a compound index is used
reasoning: A compound index is only useful for specific field combinations and ordering, but the audit marks it referenced when every indexed field appears somewhere in local usages. If field A is used in one query and field B is used in an unrelated query, the script reports the compound index as referenced even though no local query can use that compound index. This under-reports unused compound indexes and can leave schema/index bloat in place.
reproduction: Create a schema compound index on fields ["a", "b"], add one query filtering only a and another query ordering only b, then run the audit. The compound index is classified as referenced even though no query uses the pair together.
recommendation: Track per-query/per-call usage sets for where/order_by/distinct_on and only mark a compound index referenced when one usage site covers the relevant compound prefix or full field set according to the index semantics being audited.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: No tests are included for auditSchemaIndexes.mjs, and the current implementation has no fixture asserting compound-index classification behavior.
suggestedRegressionTest: Add a fixture with separate single-field usages for both fields of a compound index and assert the audit still reports the compound index as a candidate or manual-review item.
minimumFixScope: indexer-envio/scripts/auditSchemaIndexes.mjs compound usage extraction/classification, with fixture coverage for same-query versus separate-query usages.

history.note: Schema index audit now requires a compound index to be covered by one usage site rather than independent field usages; regression added.
